sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
Learn how to configure your application's connection to a MongoDB
deployment using the Node.js driver. In the following sections, you will
learn:

- [How to Create a MongoClient](node-mongoclient)

- [Specify Connection Options](node-connection-options)

- [How to Enable Network Compression](node-network-compression)

- [Enable TLS on a Connection](node-connect-tls)

- [Enable SOCKS5 Proxy Support](node-connect-socks)

- [Limit Server Execution Time](node-csot)

- [How to Connect to MongoDB Atlas from AWS Lambda](atlas/manage-connections-aws-lambda)

sections.heading: Compatibility
sections.body:
You can use the Node.js driver to connect and execute commands for
deployments hosted in the following environments:

- [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas): The fully
  managed service for MongoDB deployments in the cloud

- [MongoDB Enterprise](manual/administration/install-enterprise): The
  subscription-based, self-managed version of MongoDB

- [MongoDB Community](manual/administration/install-community): The
  source-available, free-to-use, and self-managed version of MongoDB

To learn more about performing common CRUD operations in the Atlas UI for deployments hosted in MongoDB
Atlas, see Create, View, Update, and Delete Documents.

For information about authenticating to MongoDB,
see the [authentication](node-authentication-mechanisms) section.
